description: Fix stale coordination session cleanup in hex-hub dashboard

specs.description: Sessions with no heartbeat for 60s are marked stale
specs.given: A coordination session has last_heartbeat timestamp
specs.then: Session status is marked as 'stale' in hub state

specs.description: Stale sessions older than cleanup threshold are removed
specs.given: A session has been marked stale for more than 5 minutes
specs.then: Session is removed from SQLite and no longer appears in dashboard

specs.description: Dead PIDs are marked stale immediately
specs.given: A session has a PID that no longer exists
specs.when: Hub validates PID using process existence check
specs.then: Session is marked stale regardless of last_heartbeat timestamp

domain_conventions.dead_pid_handling: Immediate stale marking, no grace period

specs.description: Dashboard shows actual agent/task counts from ICoordinationPort
specs.then: Agent and task counts reflect actual running processes, not stale SQLite cache

specs.description: Manual cleanup button removes all stale sessions immediately
specs.given: Dashboard UI has a cleanup button
specs.then: All stale sessions are removed and count is displayed (e.g., 'Removed 16 stale sessions')

specs.description: Active sessions continue to receive heartbeats
specs.then: Hub receives heartbeat and updates last_heartbeat timestamp

specs.description: Active sessions are NOT cleaned up even if older than 5 minutes
specs.given: A session has been running for 10 minutes with regular heartbeats
specs.then: Session is NOT removed because last_heartbeat is recent

domain_conventions.protection: Only stale sessions (60s+ since last heartbeat) are candidates for cleanup

specs.description: Session lifecycle events are logged to hub
specs.when: Session is registered, heartbeat received, marked stale, or cleaned up
specs.then: Event is logged with type (instance_registered, instance_alive, instance_stale, instance_removed) and timestamp
